forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,65 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a280
+title: Add view_id field to Perspective with legacy-compat loader
+---
+## What
+
+Foundational change: add an optional `view_id` field alongside the existing `view` (kind) field on perspectives so that perspectives can be scoped to a specific view instance, while existing perspectives that only have `view: board`/`view: grid` continue to load and behave as before (shared by kind).
+
+### Files to modify
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/perspective/add.rs` ‚Äî `AddPerspective` command struct: add `pub view_id: Option<String>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. Doc-comment it as "id of the view this perspective is scoped to; None means legacy shared-by-kind."
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/perspective/mod.rs` (or wherever `Perspective` is defined ‚Äî confirm via `code_context get symbol Perspective`): add the same `view_id: Option<String>` field with the same serde attrs. Update all constructors that take `view` to accept `view_id`. Keep `view: String` (kind) for backwards-compat.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/perspective.yaml` ‚Äî declare `view_id` argument on relevant verbs (at minimum `add`, `update`). Use the existing `view_kind` argument pattern in the same file as the model.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.ts` ‚Äî extend `PerspectiveDef`:
+  ```ts
+  export interface PerspectiveDef {
+    readonly id: string;
+    readonly name: string;
+    /** Legacy: view kind ("board", "grid"). Retained for backwards compat. */
+    readonly view: string;
+    /** New: id of the specific view this perspective is scoped to.
+     *  Optional ‚Äî when absent, perspective is shared across all views of `view` kind. */
+    readonly view_id?: string;
+    // ... rest unchanged
+  }
+  ```
+
+### Compatibility rule
+
+Document this rule in the `Perspective` struct doc-comment AND the `PerspectiveDef` TS docstring:
+
+> When `view_id` is `Some(id)`, the perspective belongs to exactly that view instance. When `view_id` is `None`, the perspective is **legacy-shared**: it appears in every view whose kind matches `view`. Newly created perspectives always set `view_id`; legacy `view_id`-less perspectives keep working unchanged until re-saved.
+
+No behavior changes in this task. The filter call sites still ignore `view_id`. Subsequent tasks switch the filters.
+
+### Out of scope (handled by dependent tasks)
+
+- Switching backend `perspective_commands.rs` and `dynamic_sources.rs` filters to use `view_id` ‚Äî see follow-up task "Switch backend perspective filters to view_id with kind fallback".
+- Switching `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` filter ‚Äî see "Switch frontend perspective tab bar filter to view_id with kind fallback".
+- Re-saving / migrating existing `.kanban/perspectives/*.yaml` ‚Äî see "Migrate existing perspective YAMLs to carry view_id where unambiguous".
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `Perspective` struct (Rust) and `PerspectiveDef` (TS) both expose `view_id: Option<String>` / `view_id?: string`.
+- [ ] An existing `.kanban/perspectives/*.yaml` with no `view_id:` line parses successfully and round-trips through serialize/deserialize unchanged (`view_id` stays absent ‚Äî `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`).
+- [ ] A new perspective created via `AddPerspective` with `view_id` set serializes the field into its YAML; without `view_id` set, the YAML omits the field entirely.
+- [ ] The compatibility rule (above) is captured in the struct/type doc-comment so future readers know `None` ‚áí legacy shared-by-kind.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` both pass.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Unit test in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/perspective/add.rs` (or wherever the `Perspective` loader lives): `legacy_perspective_yaml_without_view_id_loads_with_view_id_none` ‚Äî feed the loader a YAML matching `id:\nname:\nview: board\n` and assert the resulting struct has `view: "board"`, `view_id: None`.
+- [ ] Unit test: `perspective_with_view_id_round_trips` ‚Äî construct a `Perspective` with `view: "grid"`, `view_id: Some("01JMVIEW0000000000TGRID0".into())`, serialize to YAML, parse back, assert equality.
+- [ ] Unit test: `perspective_without_view_id_serializes_without_field` ‚Äî construct with `view_id: None`, serialize, assert the YAML string does NOT contain `view_id:` anywhere.
+- [ ] TS type test (if there's an existing type-only test file like `kanban.types.test.ts`, otherwise add one): a literal `{ id: "x", name: "y", view: "grid" }` satisfies `PerspectiveDef` (no `view_id` required).
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` ‚Äî green.
+- [ ] Run: `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` ‚Äî green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write the loader/round-trip unit tests first, watch them fail, then add the field and make them pass.
+- Match the existing serde-attribute style on neighboring optional fields (e.g. `AddPerspective::filter`, `AddPerspective::group`) for consistency.
\ No newline at end of file

-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/perspective/mod.rs` (or wherever `Perspective` is defined ‚Äî confirm via `code_context get symbol Perspective`): add the same `view_id: Option<String>` field with the same serde attrs. Update all constructors that take `view` to accept `view_id`. Keep `view: String` (kind) for backwards-compat.
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/perspective.yaml` ‚Äî declare `view_id` argument on relevant verbs (at minimum `add`, `update`). Use the existing `view_kind` argument pattern in the same file as the model.

-  export interface PerspectiveDef {

-    readonly name: string;

-     *  Optional ‚Äî when absent, perspective is shared across all views of `view` kind. */

When `view_id` is `None`, the perspective is **legacy-shared**: it appears in every view whose kind matches `view`. Newly created perspectives always set `view_id`; legacy `view_id`-less perspectives keep working unchanged until re-saved.
-

-

-- [ ] The compatibility rule (above) is captured in the struct/type doc-comment so future readers know `None` ‚áí legacy shared-by-kind.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` both pass.

-- [ ] Unit test: `perspective_with_view_id_round_trips` ‚Äî construct a `Perspective` with `view: "grid"`, `view_id: Some("01JMVIEW0000000000TGRID0".into())`, serialize to YAML, parse back, assert equality.

changes.value:
## What

Foundational change: add an optional `view_id` field alongside the existing `view` (kind) field on perspectives so that perspectives can be scoped to a specific view instance, while existing perspectives that only have `view: board`/`view: grid` continue to load and behave as before (shared by kind).

### Files to modify

- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/perspective/add.rs` ‚Äî `AddPerspective` command struct: add `pub view_id: Option<String>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. Doc-comment it as "id of the view this perspective is scoped to; None means legacy shared-by-kind."
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/perspective/mod.rs` (or wherever `Perspective` is defined ‚Äî confirm via `code_context get symbol Perspective`): add the same `view_id: Option<String>` field with the same serde attrs. Update all constructors that take `view` to accept `view_id`. Keep `view: String` (kind) for backwards-compat.
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/perspective.yaml` ‚Äî declare `view_id` argument on relevant verbs (at minimum `add`, `update`). Use the existing `view_kind` argument pattern in the same file as the model.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.ts` ‚Äî extend `PerspectiveDef`:
  ```ts
  export interface PerspectiveDef {
    readonly id: string;
    readonly name: string;
    /** Legacy: view kind ("board", "grid"). Retained for backwards compat. */
    readonly view: string;
    /** New: id of the specific view this perspective is scoped to.
     *  Optional ‚Äî when absent, perspective is shared across all views of `view` kind. */
    readonly view_id?: string;
    // ... rest unchanged
  }
  ```

### Compatibility rule

Document this rule in the `Perspective` struct doc-comment AND the `PerspectiveDef` TS docstring:

> When `view_id` is `Some(id)`, the perspective belongs to exactly that view instance. When `view_id` is `None`, the perspective is **legacy-shared**: it appears in every view whose kind matches `view`. Newly created perspectives always set `view_id`; legacy `view_id`-less perspectives keep working unchanged until re-saved.

No behavior changes in this task. The filter call sites still ignore `view_id`. Subsequent tasks switch the filters.

### Out of scope (handled by dependent tasks)

- Switching backend `perspective_commands.rs` and `dynamic_sources.rs` filters to use `view_id` ‚Äî see follow-up task "Switch backend perspective filters to view_id with kind fallback".
- Switching `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` filter ‚Äî see "Switch frontend perspective tab bar filter to view_id with kind fallback".
- Re-saving / migrating existing `.kanban/perspectives/*.yaml` ‚Äî see "Migrate existing perspective YAMLs to carry view_id where unambiguous".

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Perspective` struct (Rust) and `PerspectiveDef` (TS) both expose `view_id: Option<String>` / `view_id?: string`.
- [ ] An existing `.kanban/perspectives/*.yaml` with no `view_id:` line parses successfully and round-trips through serialize/deserialize unchanged (`view_id` stays absent ‚Äî `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`).
- [ ] A new perspective created via `AddPerspective` with `view_id` set serializes the field into its YAML; without `view_id` set, the YAML omits the field entirely.
- [ ] The compatibility rule (above) is captured in the struct/type doc-comment so future readers know `None` ‚áí legacy shared-by-kind.
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` both pass.

## Tests

- [ ] Unit test in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/perspective/add.rs` (or wherever the `Perspective` loader lives): `legacy_perspective_yaml_without_view_id_loads_with_view_id_none` ‚Äî feed the loader a YAML matching `id:\nname:\nview: board\n` and assert the resulting struct has `view: "board"`, `view_id: None`.
- [ ] Unit test: `perspective_with_view_id_round_trips` ‚Äî construct a `Perspective` with `view: "grid"`, `view_id: Some("01JMVIEW0000000000TGRID0".into())`, serialize to YAML, parse back, assert equality.
- [ ] Unit test: `perspective_without_view_id_serializes_without_field` ‚Äî construct with `view_id: None`, serialize, assert the YAML string does NOT contain `view_id:` anywhere.
- [ ] TS type test (if there's an existing type-only test file like `kanban.types.test.ts`, otherwise add one): a literal `{ id: "x", name: "y", view: "grid" }` satisfies `PerspectiveDef` (no `view_id` required).
- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` ‚Äî green.
- [ ] Run: `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` ‚Äî green.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write the loader/round-trip unit tests first, watch them fail, then add the field and make them pass.
- Match the existing serde-attribute style on neighboring optional fields (e.g. `AddPerspective::filter`, `AddPerspective::group`) for consistency.
Add view_id field to Perspective with legacy-compat loader

+- Match the existing serde-attribute style on neighboring optional fields (e.g. `AddPerspective::filter`, `AddPerspective::group`) for consistency. #perspective-view-id
+
+## Implementation Notes
+
+The three new round-trip unit tests live in `swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/types.rs` (the actual home of the `Perspective` struct ‚Äî `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/perspective/mod.rs` re-exports from there). The TS type test was added as a new file `kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.test.ts` (no pre-existing `kanban.types.test.ts`).
+
+The kanban command-yaml file (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/perspective.yaml`) declares slash/keybinding commands rather than the `add`/`update` operation verbs directly, so `view_id` was added as an arg on:
+- `perspective.save` (the user-facing "save current view as a new perspective" path, which dispatches `AddPerspective`)
+- `perspective.goto` (which already declared `view_kind` ‚Äî `view_id` pairs with it for per-view-id resolution)
+
+Both `add.rs` and `update.rs` thread `view_id` through `Execute::execute`. `dispatch.rs` (`dispatch_add_perspective` and `dispatch_update_perspective`) now reads `view_id` from operation params; `UpdatePerspective.view_id` uses the same `Option<Option<String>>` tri-state shape as `filter` and `group`, so `Some(None)` clears the field back to legacy shared-by-kind.
\ No newline at end of file

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [x] `Perspective` struct (Rust) and `PerspectiveDef` (TS) both expose `view_id: Option<String>` / `view_id?: string`.
-- [x] An existing `.kanban/perspectives/*.yaml` with no `view_id:` line parses successfully and round-trips through serialize/deserialize unchanged (`view_id` stays absent ‚Äî `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`).

+- [ ] An existing `.kanban/perspectives/*.yaml` with no `view_id:` line parses successfully and round-trips through serialize/deserialize unchanged (`view_id` stays absent ‚Äî `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`).
+- [ ] A new perspective created via `AddPerspective` with `view_id` set serializes the field into its YAML; without `view_id` set, the YAML omits the field entirely.

+
+## Implementation Notes
+
+The three new round-trip unit tests live in `swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/types.rs` (the actual home of the `Perspective` struct ‚Äî `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/perspective/mod.rs` re-exports from there). +
+The kanban command-yaml file (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/perspective.yaml`) declares slash/keybinding commands rather than the `add`/`update` operation verbs directly, so `view_id` was added as an arg on:
+- `perspective.save` (the user-facing "save current view as a new perspective" path, which dispatches `AddPerspective`)
+- `perspective.goto` (which already declared `view_kind` ‚Äî `view_id` pairs with it for per-view-id resolution)
+
+Both `add.rs` and `update.rs` thread `view_id` through `Execute::execute`. `dispatch.rs` (`dispatch_add_perspective` and `dispatch_update_perspective`) now reads `view_id` from operation params; `UpdatePerspective.view_id` uses the same `Option<Option<String>>` tri-state shape as `filter` and `group`, so `Some(None)` clears the field back to legacy shared-by-kind.
\ No newline at end of file
